mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero, signaling sustained monetary accommodation. This low-rate regime compresses equity risk premiums and reduces discount rates on future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declines below 5% threshold, signaling labor market tightening and wage pressure. This triggers expectations of future Fed tightening and inflation, causing equity valuations to compress. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and discount rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.0% as of 2016-10, well above 5% threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates month-over-month, indicating robust consumer demand and economic momentum. This signals sustained corporate earnings growth and reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or correlation is not significantly positive (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines for two consecutive months, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side contraction. This reduces near-term earnings expectations for cyclical sectors and increases recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: supply-side contraction or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply while simultaneously the unemployment rate rises above 5%, signaling joint deterioration in housing demand and labor market conditions. This interaction indicates weakening aggregate demand and increased recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth and earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 549.0K as of 2016-09, UNRATE at 9.0% as of 2016-10
regime_hint: demand shock or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST decline × UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates while simultaneously inflation (CPI) remains subdued below 2.5%, signaling a Phillips curve regime where labor market strength does not yet trigger wage-driven inflation. This allows the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND 12-month CPI growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131028.0K as of 2016-10, CPIAUCSL at 224.433 as of 2016-10
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum and reduced corporate earnings growth. This triggers a downward repricing of equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market adjusts to lower growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, signaling a real interest rate compression that threatens future monetary tightening. This creates uncertainty about the Fed's policy path and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure ahead of anticipated rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 224.433 as of 2016-10, FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2016-10
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization pressure with low rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPI acceleration × low rates) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
